content:
Key Findings

  • Pendle Borough Council's total analysed spending from 2021/22 to 2025/26 amounts to £129.1M.
  • The top 5 suppliers account for £37.9M of this spend, representing 29.3% of the total.
  • The single largest supplier, LIBERATA UK LTD, received £17.2M, which is 13.4% of the council's total expenditure.
  • The top 10 suppliers combined received £51.1M, making up 39.6% of the council's spending.
  • The remaining 3,009 suppliers shared £77.9M, or 60.4% of the total spend.

Understanding Pendle's Spending Landscape

Pendle Borough Council's financial records from 2021/22 to 2025/26 show a total expenditure of £129.1M. This significant sum was distributed among 3,019 unique suppliers over the five-year period. Understanding where these funds are allocated, particularly to the largest contractors, is crucial for assessing value for money for Pendle taxpayers. AI DOGE's analysis focuses on these high-value contracts to provide transparency on the council's major financial commitments.

A notable aspect of Pendle's spending is the concentration of funds with a relatively small number of key suppliers. The top 5 suppliers alone account for £37.9M of the total spend, representing 29.3%. Expanding this view, the top 10 suppliers combined received £51.1M, which is a substantial 39.6% of the council's overall expenditure. This highlights the importance of scrutinising these major contracts to ensure they deliver efficient and effective services.

The Major Contractors

The largest single recipient of Pendle Borough Council funds is LIBERATA UK LTD, with payments totalling £17.2M. This represents 13.4% of the council's entire analysed spend. Companies like Liberata often provide critical back-office services, such as finance, HR, or IT, which are essential for council operations. The scale of this contract makes it a primary area for public interest and oversight regarding service quality and cost-effectiveness.

Following Liberata, other significant suppliers include PENDLE LEISURE LTD, which received £6.7M, accounting for 5.2% of the total spend. As a leisure provider, this entity likely manages public leisure facilities and programmes within the borough. LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL also features prominently, receiving £5.9M, or 4.6%. This inter-council payment often relates to shared services, joint initiatives, or contributions for county-wide services that benefit Pendle residents.

To provide a clearer picture of the council's major financial commitments, the table below details the top 10 suppliers by total spend:

Rank Supplier Amount % of Total Spend
1 LIBERATA UK LTD £17.2M 13.4%
2 PENDLE LEISURE LTD £6.7M 5.2%
3 LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL £5.9M 4.6%
4 DEPARTMENT FOR BUSINESS AND TRADE £4.5M 3.5%
5 PEARL 2 £3.6M 2.8%
6 BRENTWOOD BOROUGH COUNCIL £3.0M 2.3%
7 SURREY COUNTY COUNCIL £3.0M 2.3%
8 A&G LANDSCAPES & GROUNDWORKS LTD £2.7M 2.1%
9 NW RECRUITMENT SERVICES LTD £2.3M 1.8%
10 GO PLANT FLEET SERVICES LTD £2.3M 1.8%

Diverse Spending and Public Sector Collaboration

Beyond the top three, other significant payments include DEPARTMENT FOR BUSINESS AND TRADE, receiving £4.5M (3.5%), which may relate to grants, economic development programmes, or regulatory fees. PEARL 2, with £3.6M (2.8%), is another key contractor. The presence of other local authorities like BRENTWOOD BOROUGH COUNCIL and SURREY COUNTY COUNCIL, each receiving £3.0M (2.3%), suggests inter-council collaboration or shared service agreements, potentially for specialised functions or procurement consortia.

While the top 10 suppliers account for a substantial £51.1M, or 39.6% of the total spend, it is also important to note the distribution across the remaining suppliers. The other 3,009 unique suppliers collectively received £77.9M, representing 60.4% of the total expenditure. This indicates that while a significant portion of the council's budget is tied up in major contracts, a larger number of smaller transactions support a broad range of local businesses and services, contributing to the local economy.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Pendle Borough Council about the details and performance metrics of the contracts with LIBERATA UK LTD or PENDLE LEISURE LTD.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to hear discussions on council spending and contract awards.
  • Check Pendle Borough Council's published contracts register for more information on specific agreements with suppliers.
  • Write to your local councillor about your concerns regarding the value for money of major council contracts and how they benefit the community.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Pendle Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Pendle Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
